location: Dropped by the Deathbird after it's defeated at night on the wall north of Castle Morne, where the wall meets the coast, in the Weeping Peninsula.
tactics: The Deathbird only appears at night — rest at a grace and wait for nightfall if it isn't there yet
A non-respawning Archer Golem in the area fires arrows that can hit both you and the bird; use it as extra chip damage but stay aware it isn't picky about targets
It's weak to Strike and Holy damage — a Holy-infused weapon, the Order's Blade incantation, or Holy Water Pots all speed the fight up considerably
lore: A hatchet used in an ancient rite of sacrifice, etched with the image of a Deathbird worshipped as a malevolent deity. The rite's old power still lingers, feeding the wielder a trickle of focus points whenever something nearby dies.

builds.name: FP-sustain quality build
builds.why: Restores FP whenever any nearby creature dies, not just the wielder's own kills — a handy passive for builds using FP-cost skills or spells without heavy Mind investment.

builds.name: Early quality pick
builds.why: Even D/D scaling and moderate requirements make it a solid Weeping Peninsula-tier axe upgrade for a Strength or Dexterity leaning build.
